rulings.Case Title: JOHN BAUER et al VS. ST. FRANCIS MEMORIAL HOSPITAL et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 19, 2010, line 1. DEFENDANT ANNE-MARIE REGAL'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. No opposition filed. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default, Default Judgemnt And For Leave To Defend The Action
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 19, 2010, line 2. DEFENDANT EVERT GROBBELAAR'S Motion To Set Aside Default, Default Judgment And For Leave To Defend The Action is granted. Within 10 days of notice of entry of this order, defendant to file an answer complying with the terms of the previous demurrer and order: pleading facts to support the alleged defenses. Defendant's former counsel, Brant Girard, to pay reasonable attorney's fees and costs associated with the default, collection efforts and response to this motion in the amount of $13,514.30. (C.C.P. section 473(b)). =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: DENNIS LIU VS. SAN FRANCISCO GENERAL HOSPITAL et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 19, 2010, line 6. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is overruled, 10 days to answer. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: LEGALMATCH.COM VS. LUIS A. GUERRA et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 19, 2010, line 7. DEFENDANTS LUIS GUERRA, KIMBERLY SCHULTZ'S Motion To Dismiss. The court treats this motion as a demurrer. The court sustains this demurrer with 10 days leave to amend as to all causes of action. Plaintiff must state what causes of action are alleged against which defendant. Plaintiff must plead whether any alleged contract was written, oral, or implied by conduct, attach a copy of the agreement to the amended complaint, or set out the terms of the contract in the body of the complaint. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: PRIVATE MEDIA GROUP, INC., et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs' Order To Show Cause To Defendant Re: Preliminary Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 19, 2010, line 9. Plaintiffs' Order To Show Cause To Defendant Re: Preliminary Injunction is denied. Plaintiffs fail to show irreparable harm or liklihood of success on the merits. The balance of equities favors defendant. =(302/CWW)
